prompt: Create a hyper-realistic cinematic vertical action photograph of a shirtless muscular man climbing a jagged icy mountain cliff during a ferocious blizzard, shot from a dramatic wide-angle perspective as if from a camera attached near the climbing rope. The man is in the lower foreground, leaning upward with intense survival energy, one hand gripping a thick rope that runs toward the viewer and the other hand holding a battered ice axe. He wears only torn, dark grey denim pants with ripped knees; his bare torso is heavily muscled, dirty, scratched, wet, and dusted with snow and frost. Behind and above him, an enormous snow leopard leaps down from the cliff face in mid-attack, mouth open snarling, blue-gray eyes focused on the climber, one front paw extended with claws visible. The scene is set in a steep Himalayan-style gorge with dark rocky walls, snow-covered ridges, icy mist, wind-blown powder, and dense diagonal snowfall. Use cold blue-gray color grading, high contrast, gritty textures, realistic fur detail, frozen skin texture, motion blur in the snow, and dramatic cinematic lighting. Keep the composition tense and dynamic: the climber occupies the lower left/center foreground, the rope creates strong depth toward the bottom right, and the attacking animal dominates the upper right background. No text, no watermark, no extra people, no modern climbing gear besides the rope and ice axe.
